response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software developer with strong full-stack and D/TypeScript/Node.js background, but the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and substantial large-scale distributed-systems/cloud-compute platform experience. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~7 months in 2014-2015) and no evidence of leading cloud compute platforms or distributed infrastructure at the required scale. Skills listed do not include C++ or cloud platforms (AWS CDK appears once but no GCP/AWS compute platform leadership). Does not meet the minimum qualifications.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with senior/staff titles and mentoring/teaching experience (workshops, courses, talks).
Demonstrated leadership of open-source projects (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts) showing ownership of ambiguous, long-running technical work.
Exposure to low-latency, high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second.
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum — only a short C/C++ role at National Instruments in 2014-2015.
No clear experience building or leading an internal cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed cloud infrastructure.
Primary tech stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not aligned with the C++-centric platform role.

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale distributed systems leadership
Senior engineering breadth and mentoring/leadership track record
